text.title: Segment-Aware Design System Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the segment-aware-design-system methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Segments MUST be modeled as a token dimension (alongside theme + density), not as forked component files.
rule.rationale: Forked files explode component count and break design-system updates; a dimension stays one source of truth.

rule.statement: Component segment variants MUST be selected via an explicit `segment` prop, not via feature flags or implicit context inference.
rule.rationale: Implicit selection makes Storybook unreviewable and a/b tests untestable.

rule.statement: Copy variants MUST live in i18n bundles keyed by (locale, segment), NOT inside component code.
rule.rationale: Copy mixed into components cannot be reviewed by content teams and breaks i18n pipelines.

rule.statement: Number of supported segments MUST be ≤ 5; more requires a research-backed merge plan.
rule.rationale: Token + copy combinatorics blow up; >5 segments is a sign the segmentation is too granular.

rule.statement: If product serves a single segment OR segments diverge functionally, skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: Single-segment products don't benefit from the dimension; functional divergence needs multi-tenancy, not a design system.
